question: Does the published BTC MVRV Z-score cycle-timing rule survive an independent Coin Metrics source, a no-lookahead expanding standard deviation, next-day execution, realistic costs, and a fair same-start 1x buy-and-hold benchmark?
decision_use: Decide whether MVRV can support a later BTC macro-cycle state model; this replication is not a product strategy candidate and has no Demo or trading effect.
strongest_claim_if_all_gates_pass: The fixed MVRV rule shows repeatable historical timing consistency across the observed cycles under this independent definition; it does not prove a causal relation, that the present bear market has ended, or that the next cycle will outperform buy-and-hold.
known_exposure: The peer-reviewed paper's positive summary and fixed -0.2 entry plus 5/6/7 exit rules were read before preregistration. Halpha has also inspected BTC price and MVRV history. Exact results under expanding standardization, fair same-start buy-and-hold, costs and the complete 2025 peak window have not been run.

fixed_definition.primary_z_score: (market cap - realized cap) / expanding standard deviation of market cap through that day, minimum 365 observations
fixed_definition.lookahead_diagnostic_only: the same numerator divided by the full available sample standard deviation
fixed_definition.entry: cross from at or above -0.2 to below -0.2
exits: cross from below 5 to at or above 5
cross from below 6 to at or above 6
cross from below 7 to at or above 7

benchmark: 1x BTC buy-and-hold with the same phase start, initial capital and entry cost; strict bull diagnostics additionally compare a hypothetical perfect next-day purchase immediately after each ex-post trough.
development_gate: at least two variants beat same-start buy-and-hold in Base total return and have shallower maximum drawdown
all three variants enter within plus or minus 180 days of both development troughs
at least two variants exit within plus or minus 180 days of both development peaks
the direction of the total-return comparison with buy-and-hold is consistent across exit thresholds 5, 6 and 7
confirmation_gate: at least two variants beat same-start buy-and-hold in Base total return
at least two variants enter near the 2022 trough and exit near the 2025 peak within plus or minus 180 days
strict 2022-trough-to-2025-peak candidate and perfect next-day buy-and-hold returns are reported without relabeling risk-adjusted superiority as raw outperformance
stop_rule: Any development failure keeps confirmation unopened. The 2025 exit threshold cannot be lowered after viewing results, and a diagnostic full-sample Z-score cannot replace the no-lookahead primary definition.
allowed_fixes: Only data-integrity, parsing, timestamp, deterministic calculation, serialization or documentation fixes that do not change the definitions, rules, periods, cycle windows, costs, benchmark or gates; record before rerun.

post_result_integrity_amendments.exposure: The first development output had already failed the frozen peak-exit timing gate; its SHA-256 was 2e6873658f2041b4ecf1b13dc36370309ca76cb44c845ab70c1b0a1d31c577f0. Confirmation remained unopened.
post_result_integrity_amendments.reason: The report listed repeated threshold crossings that vectorbt did not execute while already in or out of the position, and its strict bull slice included the trough-to-next-day return for the candidate while the stated comparator began at the next-day action price.
post_result_integrity_amendments.scope: Deterministically collapse signals to executed 0x/1x state transitions and align the candidate strict-bull slice to begin after the same next-day action close. Thresholds, portfolio returns, development benchmark, periods, costs, gates and stop rule are unchanged.
